statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: stdin contains two sorted arrays: array1[1..n] and array2[1..n]
statement.notes: n <= 10^4, no duplicate elements. Sort in ascending order.

hints.text: In the merge function, ensure that the swapping logic is correct.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> mergeSort(vector<int>& arr1, vector<int>& arr2) { if (arr1.size() >= arr2.size()) { vector<int> merged; for (int i : arr1) merged.push_back(i); for (int i : arr2) merged.push_back(i); return merged; } else { vector<int> merged; for (int i : arr2) merged.push_back(i); for (int i : arr1) merged.push_back(i); return merged; } } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public static int[] mergeSort(int[] arr1, int[] arr2) { if (arr1.length >= arr2.length) { int[] merged = new int[arr1.length + arr2.length]; System.arraycopy(arr1, 0, merged, 0, arr2.length); System.arraycopy(arr2, 0, merged, arr1.length, arr1.length); return merged; } else { int[] merged = new int[arr2.length + arr1.length]; System.arraycopy(arr2, 0, merged, 0, arr1.length); System.arraycopy(arr1, 0, merged, arr2.length, arr2.length); return merged; } } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def mergeSort(self, arr1, arr2): if len(arr1) >= len(arr2): return arr1 + arr2 else: return arr2 + arr1
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function mergeSort(arr1, arr2) { if (arr1.length >= arr2.length) { var merged = arr1.concat(arr2); return merged; } else { var merged = arr2.concat(arr1); return merged; } }

functionSignature.python: def mergeSort(self, arr1: list[int], arr2: list[int]) -> list[int]:
